headline: Tournaments get an admin-set estimated purse that fills in wherever the official ESPN purse is missing

points.text: nullable `estimatedPurse` column on [Tournament](#diff-1), set from the [admin tournament form](#diff-2) via `admin.updateTournament`
`syncLiveStandings` now [falls back to the estimate](#diff-19) for projected earnings instead of bailing with 0 players when ESPN has no purse
new [`formatPurse`](#diff-8) helper renders estimates as `~$20,000,000` on the home, dashboard, league, and tournament pages
ESPN sync still writes only `purse`, and `purse` always wins — the estimate never overwrites or masks a confirmed value
the fallback and precedence rules are pinned by four [sync tests](#diff-18)

facets.what: Add an estimated purse to tournaments, used for live earnings projections and page display until ESPN publishes the real one
facets.why: ESPN often has no purse until tournament week — pages showed nothing and live projected earnings were silently skipped
facets.size: 21 files, +102/−34 — roughly half of it tests and fixture updates

risk.note: additive nullable column; official purse always takes precedence, so behavior is unchanged wherever a real purse exists

diagram.title: Effective purse resolution — the one rule applied everywhere
diagram.d2:
direction: right
"official purse from ESPN?" -> "show purse, project earnings": yes
"official purse from ESPN?" -> "estimatedPurse set by admin?": no
"estimatedPurse set by admin?" -> "show ~estimate, project earnings": yes
"estimatedPurse set by admin?" -> "hide purse, skip projections": no
"show ~estimate, project earnings": { class: added }

title: Feature home — how the estimate flows through the stack
Schema changes
d2:
Tournament: {
  shape: sql_table
  class: changed
  "id": "String"
  "espnId": "String?"
  "season": "Int"
  "name": "String"
  "course": "String?"
  "city": "String?"
  "country": "String?"
  "startDate": "DateTime"
  "endDate": "DateTime"
  "purse": "Int?"
  "status": "TournamentStatus"
  "completedAt": "DateTime?"
  "excluded": "Boolean"
  "imageUrl": "String?"
  "updatedAt": "DateTime"
  "fields": "TournamentField[]"
  "results": "TournamentResult[]"
  "picks": "Pick[]"
  "notificationLogs": "NotificationLog[]"
  "estimatedPurse": "Int?  (+ added)"
}

procedures.name: admin.updateTournament
procedures.input:
z.object({
        id: z.string(),
        name: z.string().optional(),
        course: z.string().optional(),
        startDate: z.string().optional(),
        endDate: z.string().optional(),
        purse: z.number().int().nullable().optional(),
        estimatedPurse: z.number().int().nullable().optional(),
        status: z
          .enum(["UPCOMING", "IN_PROGRESS", "FINALIZING", "COMPLETED", "CANCELLED"])
          .optional(),
      })

title: The foundation — one nullable column
description: The entire data-model footprint of the feature: a second, deliberately separate purse column so the admin's guess and ESPN's confirmed value can never clobber each other.

blocks.title: schema.prisma
blocks.description:
**A separate `estimatedPurse Int?` beside `purse` — not a flag on the existing column.**

- `estimatedPurse Int? @map("estimated_purse")` on the `Tournament` model
- keeping it separate means ESPN sync keeps writing `purse` untouched, and precedence is a pure read-time rule
- nullable with no default — every existing row and code path behaves exactly as before until an admin fills it in

blocks.title: migration.sql
blocks.description: **Purely additive `ALTER TABLE` — nullable column, no backfill, no default.**

title: The core behavior — live projections fall back to the estimate
description: The real payoff of the PR: `syncLiveStandings` used to silently skip projected earnings whenever ESPN had no purse. Now the admin's estimate keeps live money on the board during tournament week.

blocks.title: sync.ts
blocks.description:
**The behavioral heart — projections no longer die when ESPN has no purse.**

- `syncLiveStandings` previously returned `playerCount: 0` inside the catch the moment the ESPN purse fetch failed — live projected earnings simply didn't happen
- now the catch only logs; `effectivePurse = purse ?? tournament.estimatedPurse` decides afterwards, and the sync bails only when *both* are null
- `calculateEarnings(effectivePurse, results)` projects payouts off the estimate, so members see live earnings mid-tournament
- `SyncFieldsResult.purse` is renamed `effectivePurse` with a doc comment — the value can now be an estimate, and the name says so

Reads the column selected by [the sync repository](#diff-11).

diagram.title: syncLiveStandings with the estimated-purse fallback
diagram.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  cron->>sync: syncLiveStandings(id)
  sync->>db: findTournamentForSync (purse, estimatedPurse)
  sync->>ESPN: getResults(espnId)
  ESPN-->>sync: live leaderboard
  sync->>ESPN: getEventDetails(espnId) for purse
  ESPN-->>sync: fails — purse not published
  sync->>sync: effectivePurse = purse ?? estimatedPurse
  sync->>db: upsert projected earnings from calculateEarnings

lines: +  /** The confirmed ESPN purse, or estimated purse as fallback. */

hunks.header: @@ -74,7 +75,7 @@ export async function syncFields(tournamentId: string): Promise<SyncFieldsResult

lines: console.warn(`[sync] Could not fetch purse for ${tournament.name}:`, e);
+  // Fall back to estimated purse if ESPN purse is still unavailable

blocks.title: sync.ts (repository)
blocks.description: **`findTournamentForSync` now selects `estimatedPurse`** — so [the sync service](#diff-19) can fall back without a second query.

title: The write path — how an admin sets the estimate
description: Form field → tRPC input → service → repository patch: the standard router/service/repository chain widened by one optional field, plus the admin UI that makes ownership of each value obvious.

blocks.title: tournament-admin.tsx
blocks.description:
**The admin form gains an "Estimated Purse" field — and clarifies who owns which value.**

- new controlled `estimatedPurse` input, submitted through [`admin.updateTournament`](#diff-13) as an int or `null` (clearing is possible)
- the old "Purse ($) (optional)" label becomes "Purse ($) (from ESPN)" with placeholder "Set by ESPN sync" — signaling admins normally shouldn't type there
- the read-only info panel shows `~$X` when only the estimate exists, and "Not set" when neither does

blocks.title: admin.ts (router)
blocks.description:
**The tRPC input accepts the estimate.**

- `estimatedPurse: z.number().int().nullable().optional()` on `admin.updateTournament` — same shape as `purse`
- `nullable()` lets an admin clear a stale estimate once the real purse lands

blocks.title: admin.ts (service)
blocks.description: **Service signature pass-through** — `adminService.updateTournament` forwards `estimatedPurse` to [the repository](#diff-9); the router's zod already validated it.

blocks.title: admin.ts (repository)
blocks.description:
**The admin repository reads and writes the new column.**

- `findTournaments` and `findTournamentById` select `estimatedPurse` so the admin pages can show it
- `updateTournament` accepts `estimatedPurse?: number | null` in its patch type

blocks.title: page.tsx (admin list)
blocks.description: **Admin list shows the three-way purse state** — `$X` confirmed, `~$X` estimated, `—` neither.

title: The read path — one formatter, four pages, and the repos that feed them
description: A single `formatPurse` helper enforces the `~` convention everywhere users see a purse, replacing what would otherwise be four inline ternaries; two repository selects plumb the column to those pages.

blocks.title: format.ts
blocks.description:
**`formatPurse` — the precedence rule as one shared helper.**

- confirmed `purse` → `formatCurrency(purse)`, no prefix
- else `estimatedPurse` → `~`-prefixed currency
- else `null`, which doubles as the render guard in `{formatPurse(t) && …}`
- centralizing it keeps the `~` convention identical across the home, dashboard, league, and tournament pages

lines: +export function formatPurse(tournament: { purse: number | null; estimatedPurse: number | null }): string | null {
export function formatDate(date: Date | string): string {

blocks.title: page.tsx (tournament)
blocks.description:
**Tournament page adopts `formatPurse` — plus one nuance for the pick module.**

- the header purse badge switches to [`formatPurse`](#diff-8)
- `DashboardPickModule` receives `purse: tournament.purse ?? tournament.estimatedPurse ?? null` — the module just needs *a* number, so the estimate passes through untagged

lines: +import { formatCurrency, formatDate, formatPosition, formatPurse, formatScore } from "@/lib/format";

blocks.title: page.tsx (dashboard)
blocks.description: **Dashboard swaps the raw `tournament.purse` render for [`formatPurse`](#diff-8)** — the helper's `null` return keeps the existing conditional-render pattern intact.

lines: +import { formatCurrency, formatDate, formatPosition, formatPurse, formatScore } from "@/lib/format";

blocks.title: page.tsx (league)
blocks.description: **League page: same swap** for the live-week meta line (`Purse: ~$X`).

lines: +import { formatCurrency, formatDate, formatPosition, formatPurse, formatScore } from "@/lib/format";

blocks.title: page.tsx (home)
blocks.description: **Home page: same swap** for the upcoming-tournament aside.

blocks.title: tournament.ts (repository)
blocks.description: **`estimatedPurse` joins the shared `COLUMNS` list** — every tournament read now carries the field, which is what lets the pages above call `formatPurse`.

blocks.title: pick.ts (repository)
blocks.description: **`findAllSeasonPicks` selects `tournament.estimatedPurse`** so season pick-history rows carry the field too.

title: Tests — the fallback pinned, fixtures widened
description: New sync tests pin the fallback and precedence contract; the other four files just add `estimatedPurse: null` to tournament fixtures so mocks match the widened row type.

blocks.title: sync.test.ts
blocks.description:
**Four tests pin the fallback contract.**

- `syncFields` and `syncLiveStandings` each get a "falls back to estimated purse" case: `purse: null, estimatedPurse: 20000000` with ESPN down asserts `calculateEarnings` runs with `20000000`
- the old "continues without purse" tests become "neither ESPN nor estimated" — the truly-empty case still returns 0 players / null purse
- existing assertions rename `result.purse` → `result.effectivePurse`; with `purse: 25000000` set they confirm the confirmed value still wins

lines: -const TOURNAMENT = { id: "t1", name: "The Players", espnId: "401580340", purse: 25000000, season: 2026, status: "IN_PROGRESS" as const };
+const TOURNAMENT = { id: "t1", name: "The Players", espnId: "401580340", purse: 25000000, estimatedPurse: null, season: 2026, status: "IN_PROGRESS" as const };
-    it("continues without purse if ESPN details fetch fails", async () => {
+    it("falls back to estimated purse when ESPN purse unavailable", async () => {
+    it("returns null purse when neither ESPN nor estimated available", async () => {
it("throws when tournament not found", async () => {

+    it("returns 0 players when neither ESPN nor estimated purse available", async () => {
